joined_reference: it confirms fincher 's status as a film maker who artfully bends technical know-how to the service of psychological insight .  too much of it feels unfocused and underdeveloped .  a great ensemble cast ca n't lift this heartfelt enterprise out of the familiar .  prurient playthings aside , there 's little to love about this english trifle .  it moves quickly , adroitly , and without fuss ; it does n't give you time to reflect on the inanity -- and the cold war datedness -- of its premise .  it proves quite compelling as an intense , brooding character study .  bogdanovich tantalizes by offering a peep show into the lives of the era 's creme de la celluloid .  looks and feels like a project better suited for the small screen .  verbinski implements every hack-artist trick to give us the ooky-spookies .  this is a good script , good dialogue , funny even for adults .  a painfully funny ode to bad behavior .  there is a fabric of complex ideas here , and feelings that profoundly deepen them .  the overall effect is less like a children 's movie than a recruitment film for future hollywood sellouts .  as the two leads , lathan and diggs are charming and have chemistry both as friends and lovers .  the title not only describes its main characters , but the lazy people behind the camera as well .  zhang ... has done an amazing job of getting realistic performances from his mainly nonprofessional cast .  not only is undercover brother as funny , if not more so , than both austin powers films , but it 's also one of the smarter , savvier spoofs to come along in some time .  beautifully observed , miraculously unsentimental comedy-drama .  the last 20 minutes are somewhat redeeming , but most of the movie is the same teenage american road-trip drek we 've seen before - only this time you have to read the fart jokes  if you dig on david mamet 's mind tricks ... rent this movie and enjoy !  for all its impressive craftsmanship , and despite an overbearing series of third-act crescendos , lily chou-chou never really builds up a head of emotional steam .  yes , dull .  shaky close-ups of turkey-on-rolls , stubbly chins , liver spots , red noses and the filmmakers new bobbed do draw easy chuckles but lead nowhere .  the second coming of harry potter is a film far superior to its predecessor .  if you 're hard up for raunchy college humor , this is your ticket right here .  nonsensical , dull `` cyber-horror '' flick is a grim , hollow exercise in flat scares and bad acting .  a delectable and intriguing thriller filled with surprises , read my lips is an original .  thanks to scott 's charismatic roger and eisenberg 's sweet nephew , roger dodger is one of the most compelling variations on in the company of men .  there 's no emotional pulse to solaris .  a fast , funny , highly enjoyable movie .  too restrained to be a freak show , too mercenary and obvious to be cerebral , too dull and pretentious to be engaging ... the isle defies an easy categorization .  an unwise amalgam of broadcast news and vibes .

joined_reference: a pleasant enough romance with intellectual underpinnings , the kind of movie that entertains even as it turns maddeningly predictable .  it 's not original , and , robbed of the element of surprise , it does n't have any huge laughs in its story of irresponsible cops who love to play pranks .  it 's hard to imagine alan arkin being better than he is in this performance .  dazzles with its fully-written characters , its determined stylishness ( which always relates to characters and story ) and johnny dankworth 's best soundtrack in years .  fun , flip and terribly hip bit of cinematic entertainment .  the movie fails to live up to the sum of its parts .  one of the more intelligent children 's movies to hit theaters this year .  corpus collosum -- while undeniably interesting -- wore out its welcome well before the end credits rolled about 45 minutes in .  the performances take the movie to a higher level .  one of those pictures whose promising , if rather precious , premise is undercut by amateurish execution .  a compelling spanish film about the withering effects of jealousy in the life of a young monarch whose sexual passion for her husband becomes an obsession .  uses high comedy to evoke surprising poignance .  it has all the excitement of eating oatmeal .  feature debuter d.j. caruso directs a crack ensemble cast , bringing screenwriter tony gayton 's narcotics noir to life .  the old-world - meets-new mesh is incarnated in the movie 's soundtrack , a joyful effusion of disco bollywood that , by the end of monsoon wedding , sent my spirit soaring out of the theater .  an important movie , a reminder of the power of film to move us and to make us examine our values .  it 's a bit disappointing that it only manages to be decent instead of dead brilliant .  it 's not the ultimate depression-era gangster movie .  it has the charm of the original american road movies , feasting on the gorgeous , ramshackle landscape of the filmmaker 's motherland .  the vitality of the actors keeps the intensity of the film high , even as the strafings blend together .  the movie is n't just hilarious : it 's witty and inventive , too , and in hindsight , it is n't even all that dumb .  it 's difficult to imagine the process that produced such a script , but here 's guessing that spray cheese and underarm noises played a crucial role .  while locals will get a kick out of spotting cleveland sites , the rest of the world will enjoy a fast-paced comedy with quirks that might make the award-winning coen brothers envious .  broomfield turns his distinctive ` blundering ' style into something that could really help clear up the case .  oh come on .  awesome creatures , breathtaking scenery , and epic battle scenes add up to another ` spectacular spectacle . '  paid in full is so stale , in fact , that its most vibrant scene is one that uses clips from brian de palma 's scarface .  warm water under a red bridge is a quirky and poignant japanese film that explores the fascinating connections between women , water , nature , and sexuality .  the wild thornberrys movie is a jolly surprise .  the film flat lines when it should peak and is more missed opportunity and trifle than dark , decadent truffle .  for the first time in years , de niro digs deep emotionally , perhaps because he 's been stirred by the powerful work of his co-stars .  escaping the studio , piccoli is warmly affecting and so is this adroitly minimalist movie .

joined_reference: a moody , multi-dimensional love story and sci-fi mystery , solaris is a thought-provoking , haunting film that allows the seeds of the imagination to germinate .  a celebration of quirkiness , eccentricity , and certain individuals ' tendency to let it all hang out , and damn the consequences .  harris commands the screen , using his frailty to suggest the ravages of a life of corruption and ruthlessness .  it does nothing new with the old story , except to show fisticuffs in this sort of stop-go slow motion that makes the gang rumbles look like they 're being streamed over a 28k modem .  martin and barbara are complex characters -- sometimes tender , sometimes angry -- and the delicate performances by sven wollter and viveka seldahl make their hopes and frustrations vivid .  as a first-time director , paxton has tapped something in himself as an actor that provides frailty with its dark soul .  the result is a gaudy bag of stale candy , something from a halloween that died .  it is great summer fun to watch arnold and his buddy gerald bounce off a quirky cast of characters .  the best revenge may just be living well because this film , unlike other dumas adaptations , is far more likened to a treasure than a lengthy jail sentence .  sticky sweet sentimentality , clumsy plotting and a rosily myopic view of life in the wwii-era mississippi delta undermine this adaptation .  even if you do n't think ( kissinger 's ) any more guilty of criminal activity than most contemporary statesmen , he 'd sure make a courtroom trial great fun to watch .  forced , familiar and thoroughly condescending .  this is a winning ensemble comedy that shows canadians can put gentle laughs and equally gentle sentiments on the button , just as easily as their counterparts anywhere else in the world .  scores no points for originality , wit , or intelligence .  an entertaining , colorful , action-filled crime story with an intimate heart .  manages to be sweet and wickedly satisfying at the same time .  i got a headache watching this meaningless downer .  it 's a stunning lyrical work of considerable force and truth .  i 've always dreamed of attending cannes , but after seeing this film , it 's not that big a deal .  so much facile technique , such cute ideas , so little movie .  the movie is dawn of the dead crossed with john carpenter 's ghosts of mars , with zombies not as ghoulish as the first and trains not as big as the second .  just not campy enough  when leguizamo finally plugged an irritating character late in the movie .  visually imaginative , thematically instructive and thoroughly delightful , it takes us on a roller-coaster ride from innocence to experience without even a hint of that typical kiddie-flick sentimentality .  ( e ) ventually , every idea in this film is flushed down the latrine of heroism .  the story and structure are well-honed .  for close to two hours the audience is forced to endure three terminally depressed , mostly inarticulate , hyper dysfunctional families for the price of one .  a rarity among recent iranian films : it 's a comedy full of gentle humor that chides the absurdity of its protagonist 's plight .  / but daphne , you 're too buff / fred thinks he 's tough / and velma - wow , you 've lost weight !  `` mostly martha '' is a bright , light modern day family parable that wears its heart on its sleeve for all to see .  a bloated gasbag thesis grotesquely impressed by its own gargantuan aura of self-importance ...  too slow , too long and too little happens .

joined_reference: not only are the special effects and narrative flow much improved , and daniel radcliffe more emotionally assertive this time around as harry , but the film conjures the magic of author j.k. rowling 's books .  has all the depth of a wading pool .  even in its most tedious scenes , russian ark is mesmerizing .  on this tricky topic , tadpole is very much a step in the right direction , with its blend of frankness , civility and compassion .  candid and comfortable ; a film that deftly balances action and reflection as it lets you grasp and feel the passion others have for their work .  this is human comedy at its most amusing , interesting and confirming .  in an effort , i suspect , not to offend by appearing either too serious or too lighthearted , it offends by just being wishy-washy .  you wonder why enough was n't just a music video rather than a full-length movie .  this is not the undisputed worst boxing movie ever , but it 's certainly not a champion - the big loser is the audience .  ... a fun little timewaster , helped especially by the cool presence of jean reno .  mcconaughey 's fun to watch , the dragons are okay , not much fire in the script .  there is nothing outstanding about this film , but it is good enough and will likely be appreciated most by sailors and folks who know their way around a submarine .  care deftly captures the wonder and menace of growing up , but he never really embraces the joy of fuhrman 's destructive escapism or the grace-in-rebellion found by his characters .  less dizzying than just dizzy , the jaunt is practically over before it begins .  a coarse and stupid gross-out .  serving sara does n't serve up a whole lot of laughs .  writer/director joe carnahan 's grimy crime drama is a manual of precinct cliches , but it moves fast enough to cover its clunky dialogue and lapses in logic .  this is an egotistical endeavor from the daughter of horror director dario argento ( a producer here ) , but her raw performance and utter fearlessness make it strangely magnetic .  the movie does a good job of laying out some of the major issues that we encounter as we journey through life .  a hamfisted romantic comedy that makes our girl the hapless facilitator of an extended cheap shot across the mason-dixon line .  sustains its dreamlike glide through a succession of cheesy coincidences and voluptuous cheap effects , not the least of which is rebecca romijn-stamos .  it inspires a continuing and deeply satisfying awareness of the best movies as monumental ` picture shows . '  nothing is sacred in this gut-buster .  it 's hampered by a lifetime-channel kind of plot and a lead actress who is out of her depth .  and the lesson , in the end , is nothing new .  aside from minor tinkering , this is the same movie you probably loved in 1994 , except that it looks even better .  brilliantly explores the conflict between following one 's heart and following the demands of tradition .  a rigorously structured and exquisitely filmed drama about a father and son connection that is a brief shooting star of love .  it can not be enjoyed , even on the level that one enjoys a bad slasher flick , primarily because it is dull .  a taut psychological thriller that does n't waste a moment of its two-hour running time .  a study in shades of gray , offering itself up in subtle plot maneuvers ...  the far future may be awesome to consider , but from period detail to matters of the heart , this film is most transporting when it stays put in the past .

joined_reference: if the first men in black was money , the second is small change .  charles ' entertaining film chronicles seinfeld 's return to stand-up comedy after the wrap of his legendary sitcom , alongside wannabe comic adams ' attempts to get his shot at the big time .  if the movie succeeds in instilling a wary sense of ` there but for the grace of god , ' it is far too self-conscious to draw you deeply into its world .  ... designed to provide a mix of smiles and tears , `` crossroads '' instead provokes a handful of unintentional howlers and numerous yawns .  whether you like rap music or loathe it , you ca n't deny either the tragic loss of two young men in the prime of their talent or the power of this movie .  with tightly organized efficiency , numerous flashbacks and a constant edge of tension , miller 's film is one of 2002 's involvingly adult surprises .  the minor figures surrounding ( bobby ) ... form a gritty urban mosaic .  vera 's three actors -- mollà , gil and bardem -- excel in insightful , empathetic performances .  it 's great escapist fun that recreates a place and time that will never happen again .  the son 's room is a triumph of gentility that earns its moments of pathos .  but the power of these ( subjects ) is obscured by the majority of the film that shows a stationary camera on a subject that could be mistaken for giving a public oration , rather than contributing to a film 's narrative .  it 's one pussy-ass world when even killer-thrillers revolve around group therapy sessions .  stealing harvard aspires to comedic grand larceny but stands convicted of nothing more than petty theft of your time .  light years / several warp speeds / levels and levels of dilithium crystals better than the pitiful insurrection .  the director knows how to apply textural gloss , but his portrait of sex-as-war is strictly sitcom .  so , too , is this comedy about mild culture clashing in today 's new delhi .  deliriously funny , fast and loose , accessible to the uninitiated , and full of surprises  filmmakers who can deftly change moods are treasures and even marvels .  although laced with humor and a few fanciful touches , the film is a refreshingly serious look at young women .  so devoid of any kind of intelligible story that it makes films like xxx and collateral damage seem like thoughtful treatises  the film is beautifully mounted , but , more to the point , the issues are subtly presented , managing to walk a fine line with regard to the question of joan 's madness .  makes for a pretty unpleasant viewing experience .  late marriage 's stiffness is unlikely to demonstrate the emotional clout to sweep u.s. viewers off their feet .  no sophomore slump for director sam mendes , who segues from oscar winner to oscar-winning potential with a smooth sleight of hand .  it 's too self-important and plodding to be funny , and too clipped and abbreviated to be an epic .  collateral damage finally delivers the goods for schwarzenegger fans .  ... mafia , rap stars and hood rats butt their ugly heads in a regurgitation of cinematic violence that gives brutal birth to an unlikely , but likable , hero . '  detox is ultimately a pointless endeavor .  nicks , seemingly uncertain what 's going to make people laugh , runs the gamut from stale parody to raunchy sex gags to formula romantic comedy .  the band 's courage in the face of official repression is inspiring , especially for aging hippies ( this one included ) .  the mesmerizing performances of the leads keep the film grounded and keep the audience riveted .  all that 's missing is the spontaneity , originality and delight .
